summary: Rule chunk from The World of Paranoia page 39, chunk 4.

mechanics.raw_text: The political orthodoxy of Technical Services personnel is extremely critical; Internal Security is particularly diligent in monitoring their thoughts and deeds. “ech personnel are the universal “fits” of ‘Alpha Complex; when something goes wrong ‘with your disposall, communal toilet, or pet- bot, you call Tech for help. Seven or eight decades later, a surly, grease-smeared repai man may or may not show up and refuse to fix itwithout appropriate authorization forms {available from equally-surly clerks at HPD & IMC). Intelligent citizens rapidly learn that the only way to get anything fixed is bribery Because Tech is so carefully monitored by IntSec, orthodoxy is more frequently prized among. its employees than competence. Hence, getting anything fixed right i vitally impossible. Bribery sometimes works here, but intimidation generally works bette. “Technical Services. personnel are distin- ‘guished by their special coveralls, which are amply supplied with pockets for tools and ‘pare pars. They also tend to carry small tool kits that are symbolic of the delicacy of the work they do. ‘A Typical Tech Conversation Tech: Where's ya trouble? Citizen: Our dormitory’s Cruncheetym Algae + Chip dispenser is broken. See, here itis. Tech: Uh huh, Looks okay to me Citizen: But we haven't had any algae chips for weeks! And the cafeteria hasn't had anything but the blue food every since last ‘woday’s riot. Tech: You got a 174Z6a stroke 23% Gitizen: A what? Tech: A 174Z6a stroke 23. “Authorization to Repair Communal Vending Machines, Pharmaceutical Supplement Dispensers and Autogyro Foot Baths.” Like, a form. Gitizen: Uh... no. ‘Tech: No can do, bub, Get da form and maybe 1-come back.
